# About

Mr. Mukesh Behera is working as a Junior Library Information Officer at the Central Library, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. Prior to joining IIT Jammu, he worked at the Central Library, Indian Institute of Technology Delhi, Central Library, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu; Odisha University of Agriculture and Technology, Bhubaneswar; and Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar. He has qualified for UGC-NET in Library and Information Science. He holds a bachelor's degree from Sambalpur University with secured University Top 5th Rank; Master's degree in library and information science (MLISc) from Sambalpur University and PGDCA from KIIT.

## Research Interests

Areas of Interest: Different Metrics, R, Library Automation, RFID Technology, Author Identification, Research Support Service and Implementation of emerging technologies in library and library services.

# Academic Profile

Dr. Nilesh Kumar is a dedicated library professional with over 23 years of experience in managing and modernizing library services. Currently serving as the Deputy Librarian at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), he has played a key role in enhancing library resources and digital access for students and researchers.

He holds a PhD from Babasaheb Bhimrao Ambedkar University (BBAU), Lucknow, with research focused on content analysis of university library websites in India. He obtained his Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Mathematics from Tilka Manjhi Bhagalpur University (TMBU), Bhagalpur. Subsequently, he pursued a Bachelor's and a Master's degree in Library and Information Science from Banaras Hindu University (BHU) and qualified for the UGC-NET in Library and Information Science.

Dr. Kumar has worked with prestigious institutions like BHU, ISRO, and BBAU throughout his professional journey, contributing significantly to library development and information management. He has authored research papers, book chapters, and two books, while also organizing national seminars and workshops on library technologies. His expertise in digital library solutions and passion for mentoring young professionals make him a valuable contributor to the field of library and information science in India.

Apart from his professional commitments, he has a keen interest in poetry and story writing. He is also enthusiastic about taking on new assignments and exploring diverse creative and intellectual pursuits.

## Research Support Service

Research Support Services are here to help researchers at every step of their work, from finding resources to managing data and publishing. At IIT Jammu, these services include access to e-resources, tools for checking plagiarism, and software for managing references. The library also offers support for citation analysis, research data management, and guidance on intellectual property rights. Workshops on research methods and publishing are regularly organized to help researchers stay updated. These services are designed to make research easier and more efficient, so researchers can focus on what they do best.

### How to Publish

To publish your work, select a journal that fits your topic and review its submission guidelines. Write a clear and organized manuscript with sections like an abstract, introduction, and results, ensuring it follows the journal's rules. Submit it through the journal's online system, go through the peer-review process, make any necessary revisions, and wait for it to be accepted for publication. Here, we list out some leading publishers with publishing processes. Here we list out some leading publishers with publishing processes.

Economic and Political Weekly
Elsevier (Science Direct)
Emerald
Sage
Springer
Taylor & Francis
Wiley

### Literature Review

A Literature Review is a way to explore what’s already been studied about your research topic. Through these sites, you can find relevant articles, books, and other resources to help you understand key ideas, discover gaps in the research, and build a solid base for your work. It’s a great place to start when entering into your research. The listed platforms are excellent starting points for navigating the scholarly landscape across diverse subject areas. Here are some important Literature Review site that would be beneficial for researchers.

Google Scholar
JGATE
JSTOR
SCOPUS
SSRN
Web of Science

### Thesis & Dissertations

Theses and Dissertations are comprehensive academic documents that present original research or findings in a specific field of study, typically written by graduate or doctoral students as part of their degree requirements. These works are essential for advancing knowledge and offering detailed insights into various academic subjects. You can access various theses and dissertations from institutions worldwide through specific online platforms and databases. Here are some important Theses and Dissertations site that would be beneficial for researchers.

NDLTD
PQDT Global
Shodhganga

### Writing Assistant

A writing assistant is a tool that helps improve your writing by offering suggestions for grammar, style, and clarity. It can check spelling, punctuation, and sentence structure, making writing more clear and effective. Some tools also help with plagiarism checks and tone adjustments. It’s a handy resource for anyone looking to improve their writing. Many writing assistants also offer real-time feedback, which helps in refining drafts faster. They can be especially useful for non-native English speakers, ensuring their writing meets high academic standards. Here are some important Writing Assistant.

Grammarly
Turnitin
QuillBot

### Reference Management Tools

Reference management tools make it easy to keep track of your research sources and create bibliographies in different citation styles. They help streamline the process of organizing references, saving valuable time during the writing and submission stages. Many of these tools also offer features like automatic citation generation, collaboration with co-authors, and cloud syncing for access across multiple devices. Additionally, they assist in organizing PDFs, notes, and annotations, making it easier to stay organized. Some tools also integrate directly with word processors, allowing seamless citation insertion while writing. Here are some important Reference Management software/tools that would be beneficial for researchers.

Crossref
EndNote
KnightCite
Mendeley
NoodleTools
RefWorks
Zotero
ZoteroBib

### Researcher ID

A Researcher ID is a unique ID that helps identify individual researchers and their work. It makes it easier for others to find and recognize a researcher’s contributions. By having a Researcher ID, scholars can boost the visibility of their research globally, track citations more effectively, and create a professional profile that highlights their expertise. It helps researchers connect with others in their field, build their reputation, and increase the impact of their work worldwide. Here are some important Researcher IDs that would be beneficial for researchers.

ArXiv Author IDs

ORCID ID

### Bibliographic Databases

Bibliographic databases are organized collections of references to scholarly publications, such as articles, books, and conference papers. They help researchers easily find relevant literature, track citations, and stay updated in their field. These databases also assist in identifying trends, gaps in research, and potential collaborations. By offering advanced search features, they enable researchers to refine their search and access high-quality academic resources. Here are some important Bibliographic Databases.

Google Scholar
OpenAlex
PubMed
Scopus
Web of Science

### Research Tools

Research tools are helpful resources that make the research process easier for researchers. They include things like software for analyzing data, tools for organizing references, and platforms for surveys. These tools help researchers stay organized, work more efficiently, and focus on getting accurate results. Ultimately, they save time and improve the quality of research. Here are some important Research Tools that would be beneficial for researchers.

# About Me

Dr. Sunil Shetty is a Junior Library Information Officer at IIT Jammu, with over 6 years of experience in library management and information science. He holds a PhD from Presidency University, Bangalore, specializing in Collection Development in university libraries. His qualifications include a MLISc. from Manipal Academy of Higher Education, Manipal, and he is KSET qualified in Library and Information Science. Dr. Shetty has contributed significantly to library services and innovation at institutions like Presidency University, Bangalore. Throughout his career, he has published numerous research articles and actively participated in conferences to stay at the forefront of the field.

## Central Library

The Central Library of IIT Jammu is a cornerstone of academic excellence and
Research. The library acts as a central point for information and study,
offering a thoughtfully selected assortment of books, journals, and digital
resources.  It provides a serene and conducive environment for students,
faculty and Researchers to delve into their studies and explore new
frontiers. The library’s diligent personnel are consistently prepared
to aid users in locating the necessary resources and navigating the
extensive information terrain. The Central Library at IIT Jammu is a
significant asset to the Institute’s Intellectual Community due to its
dedication to increasing knowledge and cultivating a passion for learning.

“The Library, IIT Jammu is an integral part of the Institute’s academic and
research work. As an Informatics centre of the Institute, Library provides
an enjoyable learning experience with optimum ambience for study and
learning on all working days. It provides information/knowledge resources
through a carefully developed and balanced collection of Books and Journals.
”

## Mission

To swiftly expand the library collection and enrich it with the most recent
collections in order to fulfill the library’s user needs.

## Vision

To establish it as the best Science and Technology library, by providing
exceptional infrastructure, top-notch services, and facilities.

## History (
IIT Jammu )

The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu was inaugurated on 6th August 2016,
and welcomed the first batch of students into the campus in Paloura, Jammu.
In the initial phases, the establishment of IIT Jammu was done under the
mentorship of IIT Delhi.

In 2018, IIT Jammu shifted its primary operations to the 400 acres of lush
green land in Jagti village, just outside Jammu city, provided by the
Government of Jammu and Kashmir for establishing its main campus. Currently,
Phase 1A of the main campus, spread across 25 acres, is operational. Phase
1B and 1C are undergoing rapid construction. The main campus of the
Institute is located on National Highway–44, 17 km from the Jammu Airport
and 19 km from the Jammu Tawi Railway Station.

The campus in Paloura now accommodates PhD scholars. It also houses the
high-end research facility: Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF or
SAPTARSHI Labs), equipped with highly sophisticated state-of-art instruments
to enable researchers in basic sciences and engineering.

### Previously, the Central Library was functional at the Paloura
Campus. Since 2019, it has been shifted to a large hall at the
Jagti campus. Central Library, IIT Jammu will soon be relocated
to the Lecture Theatre Complex (Phase-1C) at more spacious
location having all the well-equipped facilities.

## Academic Writing Tools

An academic writing tool aids scholars in composing, organizing, and
formatting research papers. It typically includes features for citation
management, grammar checking, and document structuring. Such tools enhance
efficiency, ensure adherence to academic standards, and facilitate
collaboration among researchers, thereby improving the quality and
presentation of scholarly work.

## Grammarly

Grammarly reviews spelling, grammar, punctuation, clarity, engagement, and
delivery mistakes in English texts, detects plagiarism, and suggests
replacements for the identified errors. It also allows users to customize
their style, tone, and context-specific language.

academic and professional writing. Common styles include APA (American
Psychological Association), MLA (Modern Language Association), Chicago, and
Harvard. Each style prescribes specific formats for citations within the
text and for listing references at the end of the document, ensuring clarity
and consistency in scholarly communication.

## Institute Repository

## D-Space

The Library features a ‘D-SPACE Digital Repository’ that serves
as a platform for hosting scholarly information such as books, book
chapters, journal articles, conference papers, presentations, and other
intellectual materials developed by the IIT Jammu Faculty & students.

DSpace is a web application, allowing researchers and scholars to publish
documents and data. While DSpace shares some feature overlap with content
management systems and document management systems, the DSpace repository
software serves a specific need as a digital archives system, focused on the
long-term storage, access and preservation of digital content.

DSpace preserves and enables easy and open access to all types of digital
content including text, images, moving images, mpegs and data sets.  And
with an ever-growing community of developers, committed to continuously
expanding and improving the software, each DSpace installation benefits from
the next.

In today's fast-paced and ever-evolving landscape of
information, our library is dedicated to fostering a culture of
innovation and research. Our Central Library offers a wide range of books,
journals and e-resources, including research
support tools based on dynamic requirements. The library understands the
needs of the users and continually adapts new
resources & services. The library also facilitates accessing these resources
from outside the campus anytime and
anywhere.

The Central Library functions on the principle of providing the right
information to the right reader at the right time.

especially in the field of Technology.

Let us create a conducive environment for the flourishing of information and

participation inspires us to constantly enrich our

Read, Discover, Innovate !

## Electronic resources
(e-resources) refer to digital content accessible via the internet,

convenient and immediate access to vast amounts of information across

## Frequently Asked Questions

### 1. How do I become a library member?

To become a library member, Kindly login to your
account in SARAL
using the provided credentials,
raise the student request with subject library
membership and attach your image.

### 2. What are the library hours?

Monday to Friday: 09:00 am - 10:00 pm

Saturday: 09:00 am – 05:30 pm (Break- 01:00 pm
to 01:30 pm)

Closed on Sundays and Government of India
holidays.

### 3. How can I borrow materials and how long can I
keep borrowed items?

First, verify the availability of a document in
the Central Library OPAC search facility or Scan
the QR code closer to the circulation desk.
Second, find the book in the open stacks for
yourself. Third, check out the books using your
Institute Identity Card at the Circulation Desk.
Circulation hours are: Monday to Friday: 09:00
am - 10:00 pm Saturday: 09:00 am – 05:30 pm
(Break- 01:00 pm to 01:30 pm)
Loan periods vary depending on the user category
and type of material. Kindly visit the Library
Policy webpage for further details or ask to
circulation staffs.

### 4. How can I renew a book and how do I return
the book?

In most cases, books can be renewed if no one
else has requested them. It can be renewed at
the Circulation Desk.
Books may be presented at the Circulation Desk
during the circulation hours. Always verify your
account to ensure return. You can renew/return
items by visiting the library in person.

### 5. What if I return books late?

Late fees may apply for overdue items as Rs.
02/- (Two) per book per day for the first 14
days for all user categories, followed by Rs.
05/- (Five).

### 6. How do I access the online resources and
e-books and can it be accessed remotely?

Online resources and e-books are usually
accessible within the campus network through the
library's website. It can be also accessed
remotely through an Off-Campus Accesslink by
using an LDAP ID and password.

### 7. Where can I go to discover answers that are
not provided here?

Ask the Library Staff, at Central Library
through an e-mail at library@iitjammu.ac.in or
contact via telephone or contact in person. For
Book Procurement: 0191-257-1106 (Mr Vishwajeet);
Circulation: 0191-257- (Mr Sunil Shetty, Mr
Vinod);
E-resources/ Periodicals/Over All Library
Activity: 0191-257-0631/9451527031 (Dr Nilesh
Kumar). Answers will be provided as soon as
possible.

## Grammarly

Grammarly is an academic Writing tool, which reviews spelling, grammar,
punctuation, clarity, engagement, and delivery mistakes in English texts,
detects plagiarism, and suggests replacements for the identified errors. It
also allows users to customize their style, tone, and context-specific
language.

- !Image

## One time Self-Registration Process – New Users

### Note:

Do not Sign up with Google / Facebook / Apple

- Step 1: Go to
https://www.grammarly.com/enterprise/signup (Mandatory to sign up
from this URL)
- Step 2: Fill up the
self-registration form and click Agree & Sign up. Ensure that users
are registering from their respective official email id
(@iitjammu.ac.in)
- Step 3: After clicking Sign up, a
verification email will be sent to the user's institutional email
ID.
- Please Note: Your @edu credential
activation has been activated, and a verification email will be sent
to the user's institutional email ID once they register. Users may
sometimes get the verification mail in the Spam/Junk/Promotion
folder.
- Step 4: Kindly Verify the
activation mail from Grammarly.
- Once registered, users will land
on the 'Grammarly Editor, ' which is easy to use and explore.
- The MS Word Plugin can be
downloaded from the support page after registration from the link
https://www.grammarly.com/office-addin/windows. The same User ID and
Password credentials will grant access to use the Plugin for
users.

## Inter Library Loan/Document Delivery
Services

Interlibrary loan (ILL) is a service offered by libraries
where they borrow materials (such as books, articles, or
other resources) from another library on behalf of their
patrons. It enables access to materials not available
locally, expanding research possibilities and supporting
academic and scholarly pursuits beyond a library’s own
collection.

The library has always taken an active part in availing the

## Library General Rules

## Library general rules typically encompass guidelines and
policies that govern behavior, usage of resources, and
facilities within the library.

Library users are requested to bring their I-Card
while coming to the Library.

No personal belongings are allowed inside the
library.

Only notepad/notebooks/laptops/pen/pencil like
stationary items are allowed inside the library.

The Library user shall not engage in audible
conversation in any part of the Library.

Refreshments of any kind shall not be taken anywhere
in the library premises.

Personal Bags should be kept at Property Counter at
own risk. No valuable items,e.g., Laptop, mobile,
money, ATM Cards, etc. should be kept inside the
bags. If kept, the Library will not be responsible
for any such loss.

The library shall not be responsible for any loss or
damage to the personal belongings of the users.

The users shall not write upon, damage or mark any
book belonging to the Library. Marking, scratching,
damaging, mutilating, stealing, library materials,
or property will invite disciplinary action against
the defaulters.

The user is responsible for any damage caused by
him/her to the books or any other property belonging
to the Library, shall be required to pay the penalty
imposed upon him/her by the Library Authority.

Books removed from the shelves, if not required
further, should be kept on the nearest table. Please
do not try to shelve them yourself. Please remember
that a book misplaced is a book lost.

The User should check the books before borrowing to
ensure that these are not damaged. If a book is
found to be damaged or certain pages are missing,
the borrower should get this statement recorded on
the book. Otherwise, he/she shall be held
responsible for the damage, found at the time of
returning the books.

Users should not talk or discuss as they may disturb
other Users. Reading areas are meant for individual
study and research only.

The Library reserves the right to call back any
issued book/item at any time.

Online chatting, Playing games, watching movie on
computers, browsing social networking sites is
strictly prohibited. Strict disciplinary action will
be taken against the defaulters.

Users are not allowed to share their ID and Password
with other students.

Users should not remove/ unplug computer cables/
connections, network cables, and other peripherals/
accessories in the library.

Users must take care of their Pen drives, CD/ DVD
ROMs, mobiles, wallets, etc. The library is not
responsible for any losses. Users should keep their
Mobile Phones on Silent/Vibration mode before
entering the library.

The users should not leave their reading material
and personal belongings on the tables unattended. If
found unattended for long-time, beyond 24 hours, it
may be removed by the Library Staff/Security staff
during the cleaning of tables and the library will
not be responsible for the same. The same will also
be disposed-off using standard procedures.

Users should not leave purse/books/copies on the
table and cannot claim, capture, and reserve seats
for future use. Capturing/reserving seats for
friends/future use is strictly prohibited.

The seats are to be used/occupied on the first-come,
first-served basis.

Users should not rest or keep their feet on tables,
chairs, shelves, etc.

Smoking, chewing and spitting in the library is
prohibited.

Membership privileges of a user can be suspended on
account of misbehavior with the Library staff or for
indecent manners. Maintaining discipline in the
broadest sense is the motto of the library.

These rules are subject to revision/change from time
to time without assigning any reason. The Librarian
is free to take any decision in the interest of the
library or institution.

Central Library, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, Jagti,
NH-44 , PO Nagrota Jammu – 181 221 J&K, India

## Library Policy

Library policy refers to guidelines governing operations, services, and
patron conduct within the library. It outlines rules for borrowing
materials, using facilities, accessing digital resources, and behavior
expectations. Policies ensure equitable access, protect resources, and
maintain a conducive environment for learning and research among diverse
library users.

## General Rules

- Library users are
requested to bring their I-Card while coming to the
Library;
- No personal
belongings are allowed inside the library;
- Only
notepaper/notebooks/laptops will be allowed inside
the library;
- Members are
requested to handle the Library reading materials
with the utmost care. Mutilation, writing,
scribbling, marking, and cutting pages will be
severely dealt with;
- The Library user
shall not engage in audible conversation in any part
of the Library;
- Use of mobile
phones in the Library is prohibited;
- Refreshments of
any kind shall not be taken anywhere in the library
premises;

- !Image
- !Image

- !Image
- !Image

## Circulation

- The Circulation
Desk handles all issues & returns of Library
materials during its working hours.
- Borrowers are
requested to make sure that the books are properly
issued or returned.
- No book in
damaged condition may be accepted by the borrower.
Mutilated or spoiled books may have to be replaced
by the borrower.
- Library materials
will be issued/returned only if the borrower shows
the I-Card at the circulation desk.
- Library tickets
are not transferable. The borrower is responsible
for the return of the books borrowed on his/her
card.
- Members are held
responsible for all items issued on their name until
the respective members return them to the
Library.
- No material from
the Library should be taken out without proper
issue/ recording. Any type of violation may lead to
disciplinary action.

## All
the students, faculty members and staff of the Institute can
register themselves for membership in the library. All
Students, faculty and staff members are issued Library
Tickets. Their entitlement, in terms of the number of books
that they can borrow, are as follows

| Member Category | No. of Books that can be issued | Duration of Issue (Days) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Faculty | 6 | 90 |
| PhD. | 5 | 30 |
| B.Tech | 2 (GS) | 14 |
| 2 (TB) | 7 |  |
| M.Tech | 3 (GS) | 14 |
| 1 (TB) | 14 |  |
| Project/ Teaching Assistant/Others | 2 | 14 |
| Staff (All Categories) | 2 | 30 |
| Hindi Book (All Members Category) | 1 | 30 |

## GS-
General Section , TB- Text Book Section

For faculty and PhD, a maximum of two times reissues
will be allowed subject to the demand of the books.

## Library Timing

- Monday to Saturday (Mansar Library)
: **9.00 AM – 05.00 AM (Next Day)**
- Saturday :
**9.00 AM - 5.30 PM (Lunch Break: 1.00 PM - 1.30
PM)**

- Sunday & Gazetted
Holiday : Closed

## Library Circulation Timings

- Monday to Friday
: **9.00 AM – 9.00 PM**

- Sunday & Gazetted
Holiday : Closed

## Cost
Recovery of Lost Books by the Library Members

## Following are the approved criteria for recovering the cost
of lost books by Library members

- The lost book
should be replaced by the latest edition (new book)
with overdue charges if any, and with processing
charges of Rs. 50/- for each book
- In case the book
is out of print or not available in the market, then
double of the cost mentioned in the Accession
register should be charged with processing charges
of Rs. 50/- for each book with overdue charges, if
any
- The cost of the
book in foreign currency is to be charged at the
current conversion rate of the currency

## Overdue Fine

- Rs. 02/- (Two)
per book per day for the first 14 days for all user
categories, followed by Rs. 05/- (Five).
- If the overdue
fine exceeds 90 days, the book will not be issued to
their account, and the account will be suspended for
the next 30 days.

## Plagiarism Detection Tools

A plagiarism detection tool identifies and highlights instances of copied or
improperly referenced content in academic or professional documents. It
compares submitted text against a vast database of existing publications and
online sources to ensure originality and academic integrity. These tools are
crucial for educators, researchers, and publishers to maintain ethical
standards in writing

iThenticate is the premier tool for researchers and professional writers to
check their original works for potential plagiarism. With an easy to use
submission process that checks for similarity against the world’s top
published works, researchers and institutions can feel confident that their
academic reputation will be protected.

## Print Resources

Print resources refer to physical materials such as books, journals,
newspapers, and magazines available in libraries. They provide in-depth
information on various topics, often complementing digital resources. Print
resources are accessed on-site and may require borrowing privileges.
Libraries curate collections to support research, learning, and recreational
reading.

## Books

Central Library-IIT Jammu has total collection of 4358 printed books and 80+
e-book titles. Different Type of collection like Text books, Reference
books, Literature books, Thesis etc. are available. Details of Available
books can be accessed using Online Public Access Catalogue, or user can
directly visit to Library during working hours. Books can also be reserved
for circulation using ***OPAC***.
Books are continuously added in our library collection based on the demands
of User. Library uses Dewey decimal classification scheme for classification
of Books. Library has empanelled 23 vendors, who supply the printed book.

Click Here

## Reference Books

The Library maintains a separate reference collection consisting of
encyclopaedias, dictionaries, handbooks, technical data, almanacs, atlases,
bibliographies, etc.

These reference books are not issued to users, due to their multiple volumes
and high cost. These are only allowed to read/refer during library hours.
These books are stacked at Reference Section of a library. Details of
reference books can be accessed using OPAC.

Click Here

## Print Journals

Central Library subscribe print Journals based on the request of the users.
Currently library has subscribed 1 print journal.

However, library has subscribed 3 newspapers in English and Hindi languages
for the readers of IIT Jammu.

## Thesis and Dissertations

Thesis is submitted by M.Tech+PhD (dual degree) and PhD students.
Dissertation is submitted by Mtech and B.Tech+M.Tech(Dual degree). Thesis
and Dissertations are accepted after final approval/confirmation of the
respective HoD only.

Currently, Central library holds 41 PhD theses. The hard copy of theses is
available in library and given to users for reference purpose only.

Click Here

## Hindi Collection

There are a lot of good Hindi books placed in the Central Library. There are
literature books in Hindi as well as books on different topics that are
taught and researched at the Institute. Books in Hindi are kept in a visible
place near the reference area in the Library to encourage people to use them
and to boost the use of Rajbhasha Hindi.

Click Here

## General Books

General books refer to non-specialized literature covering a wide range of
topics, from fiction and biographies to self-help and reference works. They
cater to diverse interests and are accessible in libraries for borrowing or
reading on-site. General books enrich knowledge, stimulate imagination, and
offer entertainment for readers of all ages.

## Academic Writing Tools

An academic writing tool aids scholars in composing,
organizing, and formatting research papers. It typically
includes features for citation management, grammar checking,
and document structuring. Such tools enhance efficiency,
ensure adherence to academic standards, and facilitate
collaboration among researchers, thereby improving the
quality and presentation of scholarly work.

Know More

## Plagiarism Detection Tools

A plagiarism detection tool identifies and highlights
instances of copied or improperly referenced content in
academic or professional documents. It compares submitted
text against a vast database of existing publications and
online sources to ensure originality and academic integrity.
These tools are crucial for educators, researchers, and
publishers to maintain ethical standards in writing.

Know More

## Citation And Reference Styles

Citation and reference styles dictate how sources are
acknowledged in academic and professional writing. Common
styles include APA (American Psychological Association), MLA
(Modern Language Association), Chicago, and Harvard. Each
style prescribes specific formats for citations within the
text and for listing references at the end of the document,
ensuring clarity and consistency in scholarly communication.

How to Register
One time Self-Registration Process – New Users:-
Step 1: Go to https://www.grammarly.com/enterprise/signup (Mandatory to sign up from this URL)
Step 2: Fill up the self-registration form and click Agree & Sign up. Ensure that users are registering
from their respective official email id (@iitjammu.ac.in)
Note: Do not Sign up with Google / Facebook / Apple
Step 3: After clicking Sign up, a verification email will be sent to the user's institutional email ID.
Please Note: Your @edu credential activation has been activated, and a verification email will be sent to
the user's institutional email ID once they register. Users may sometimes get the verification mail in
the Spam/Junk/Promotion folder.
Step 4: Kindly Verify the activation mail from Grammarly.
Once registered, users will land on the 'Grammarly Editor, ' which is easy to use and explore.
The MS Word Plugin can be downloaded from the support page after registration from the
link https://www.grammarly.com/office-addin/windows. The same User ID and Password credentials
will grant access to use the Plugin for users.
How to Use Grammarly
1. Go to URL: www.grammarly.com/edu
2. Click on Login, Enter Email ID and Password, and then you will land on the Grammarly Home Page.
3. You can create a new blank document or upload an existing document to edit it with Grammarly.
4. Document format for uploading should be Microsoft Word (.doc, .docx), OpenOffice (.odt), .txt, &
rtf.
5. Select the language type in the customize section:
https://account.grammarly.com/customize Accounts > Customize > Language
6. If you already have an existing file, upload the document or start writing a new document by
clicking New, select the document type.
7. Click on Goals to set Audience type, Formality, Domain, Tone, & Intent of the Paper you are
writing, and click on done
8. Click on Plagiarism(Plagiarism will be usually switched off, Kindly switch it on every time you log in)
– Right bottom corner.
9. Rectify all alerts/issues, click on the Overall Score (right top corner), and click on download the
pdf report.
10. The MS Word Plugin can be downloaded from the support page after registration from the
link https://www.grammarly.com/office-addin/windows; the same user ID and Password credentials
will grant access to use the Plugin for users.
